name: Grandma's Cornbread Dressing
recipeIngredient: 2 cups milk or buttermilk,  see note
2 1/2 cups hot chicken broth (or 3 cups, if needed)
1/2 teaspoon salt OR 2 chicken boullion cubes added to hot chicken broth
1/2 teaspoon black pepper, (or more, to taste)
recipeInstructions:
1. For Cornbread: Mix all ingredients and place in a well-greased 10-inch pan that has been heated in a 450 degree oven (about 4 minutes) until hot. Bake at 450 degrees for about 30 or 35 minutes, or until lightly browned. Cool; crumble cornbread into a large bowl.
2. For Dressing: Saute celery and onion in butter until tender. Combine cornbread, crackers, sauteed vegetables, and remaining ingredients, mixing well. If the dressing doesn't seem moist enough, add some hot milk - about 1 cup. Spoon into a lightly greased 13x9x2-inch baking pan or casserole dish. Bake at 450 degrees for 25 to 30 minutes until lightly browned. (For Pyrex dishes bake at 375 or 400 degrees.)
- Make this with turkey at Christmas or Thanksgiving and use Grandma's Gravy (recipe in this cookbook).
